title: Premium Dark-Mode Full-Stack Dashboard with Admin Panel

requirements.summary: Apply consistent premium dark-mode visual theme (#0B0E14 base, glassmorphism cards, indigo accents, Space Grotesk/Inter fonts) across all components
acceptanceCriteria: Root background is #0B0E14
Cards and panels use glassmorphism style (bg-white/5 or similar, backdrop-blur, border border-white/10)
Indigo is the primary accent color for buttons, active states, and badges
Tailwind config reflects these tokens globally
No light-mode elements or conflicting color schemes are visible

file_operations.description: Update Tailwind configuration to define #0B0E14 as the base background color token, add indigo accent colors as primary theme tokens, ensure Space Grotesk and Inter fonts are properly configured, and add glassmorphism-specific utilities (backdrop-blur classes, semi-transparent backgrounds with white/5 opacity).

Set the root body background to #0B0E14, update CSS custom properties to use indigo accents for interactive elements, ensure glassmorphism card styles are defined globally (e.g., .glass-card with backdrop-blur and border-white/10), and verify font imports for Space Grotesk and Inter are present.
Apply #0B0E14 background color to the main layout wrapper, ensure the content area and shell containers use the premium dark theme with proper contrast for readability.

requirements.summary: Implement responsive AppShell layout: fixed sidebar (SideNavigation) on desktop with user profile/greeting/role badge, persistent bottom navigation (BottomNavigation) on mobile, both role-aware
acceptanceCriteria: Desktop sidebar is visible on screens md and above, hidden on smaller screens
Sidebar displays user avatar, display name, a time-based greeting, and role badge
Mobile bottom nav is visible only on small screens and is always fixed to the bottom
Navigation items are role-filtered (admin/owner items only shown to those roles)
Active route is visually highlighted in both sidebar and bottom nav

file_operations.description: Enhance SideNavigation to display a time-based greeting (e.g., 'Good morning, [Name]') above or below the user profile info. Ensure the user avatar, display name, and role badge (via UserRoleBadge component) are prominently displayed. Apply glassmorphism card styling to the sidebar container. Verify the component is hidden on mobile (below md breakpoint) and visible on desktop.

Ensure BottomNavigation is fixed to the bottom of the viewport on mobile screens only (below md breakpoint), hidden on desktop. Apply glassmorphism styling (backdrop-blur, semi-transparent background) to the bottom nav bar. Verify role-filtering logic for nav items and that the active route is highlighted with the indigo accent color.

Wire SideNavigation to render conditionally on desktop (md+) and BottomNavigation to render on mobile (<md). Ensure the main content area has appropriate padding/margins to account for the fixed sidebar and bottom nav without overlap.

requirements.summary: Build Vertical Clip Preview component displaying selected/recent clip in 9:16 aspect ratio with title, time range, duration, and viral score badge
acceptanceCriteria: Preview container enforces a 9:16 aspect ratio
Clip title, start/end timestamps, and duration are displayed
Viral score badge is shown with color coding
Component renders a placeholder state when no clip is selected
Component is rendered on the main dashboard page

file_operations.description: Enhance VerticalClipPreview to enforce a strict 9:16 aspect ratio container using Tailwind aspect-ratio utilities. Display the clip thumbnail (if available) as a background image or img element. Show clip title, formatted start/end timestamps (MM:SS format), calculated duration, and a ViralScoreBadge component. Apply glassmorphism card styling with indigo accent borders. Render a placeholder state (e.g., dashed border, centered icon/text) when no clip is selected.

Wire VerticalClipPreview into the HomePage component layout. Position it prominently (e.g., in a sidebar column or featured section above the clip list). Pass the currently selected clip suggestion or the most recently created clip as props to VerticalClipPreview. Ensure it is always visible on the main dashboard view.

requirements.summary: Enhance AI caption editor (editable textarea with character count, markdown hints, glassmorphism card) and YouTube upload scheduler page (select clip, schedule datetime, list/delete scheduled uploads, channel status badge)
acceptanceCriteria: Caption editor textarea is editable with live character count
Caption editor card uses glassmorphism styling
Scheduler page lists existing scheduled uploads with delete option
Scheduler allows selecting a clip and a future datetime
YouTube channel connection status is visible on the scheduler page

file_operations.description: Enhance CaptionEditor to wrap the textarea in a glassmorphism card (backdrop-blur, bg-white/5, border-white/10). Add a live character count display below or beside the textarea. Include markdown formatting hints (e.g., placeholder text or a tooltip with examples like **bold**, *italic*). Apply indigo accent styling to focus states and borders.

Enhance Scheduler page to prominently display YouTube channel connection status badge (via ChannelConnection component or inline status indicator). Ensure the clip selection dropdown and datetime picker use glassmorphism card styling with indigo accents. Verify the scheduled uploads list shows delete buttons with confirmation dialogs. Add loading skeletons for pending data fetches. Ensure all UI elements follow the premium dark-mode theme.

requirements.summary: Build/enhance Trending in Niche creator feed page (TrendingPage) with ranked leaderboard, trend scores, thumbnails in social-feed grid, glassmorphism cards, indigo highlights for top entries
acceptanceCriteria: Trending page shows a ranked list/grid of clips with trend scores
Each clip card displays title, creator identifier, score, and rank badge
Top 3 entries are visually distinguished (e.g., gold/silver/bronze indigo accent)
Loading skeleton states are shown while data is fetching
Empty state is handled gracefully

file_operations.description: Enhance TrendingPage to render a ranked leaderboard/grid of trending clips using data from useTrendingClips and useAdminStats hooks. Each clip card should display title, creator info (via user profile or principal), trend score, and a rank badge (1st, 2nd, 3rd, etc.). Apply glassmorphism card styling to each entry. Distinguish top 3 entries with indigo accent borders or glow effects (e.g., ring-2 ring-indigo-500 for 1st place). Show loading skeleton states while data is fetching. Render an empty state with a centered message and icon when no trending clips are available.

Enhance TrendingSidebar to display top trending analytics items in a compact, visually appealing format using glassmorphism card styling. Ensure the sidebar integrates seamlessly with the TrendingPage layout (e.g., as a sticky sidebar column). Apply indigo accent highlights to top-ranked entries.

requirements.summary: Implement Manager View (ContentManager page) as full CRUD interface for content entries, accessible to manager/admin/owner roles, with glassmorphism card styling
acceptanceCriteria: Content entries are listed in a table or card grid
Each entry has Edit and Delete action buttons
A form or modal is available to add new content entries
Delete action shows a confirmation dialog before proceeding
Page is gated to manager/admin/owner roles; other users see an access denied message
All mutations use existing useContentEntries hooks

file_operations.description: Enhance ContentManager page to wrap content entry cards/table in glassmorphism styling (backdrop-blur, bg-white/5, border-white/10). Ensure each entry displays Edit and Delete action buttons with indigo accent hover states. Add a 'Create New Entry' button at the top of the page that opens a form or modal for adding new content. Implement delete confirmation dialogs using a modal or alert component. Verify role-gating logic shows an AccessDeniedScreen or similar error message for users without manager/admin/owner roles. Use existing useContentEntries hooks (useCreateContentEntry, useUpdateContentEntry, useDeleteContentEntry) for all mutations.

requirements.summary: Build Admin Panel (AdminPanel component) as gated dashboard section (admin/owner only) with: User & Clip Management (searchable user table, Ban/Unban, batch clip deletion), System Controls (restart/shutdown buttons with confirmation), Analytics (activity log table, click tracking, Videos Per Hour chart), Support Messaging (direct user message form)
acceptanceCriteria: Admin panel route/page is only accessible to admin and owner roles
User management table lists all users with current status and role, with Ban/Unban toggle buttons
Batch deletion UI allows selecting multiple clips associated with a user and deleting them
System Controls section shows Restart and Shutdown buttons, each with a confirmation dialog before calling the backend
Activity log table auto-refreshes every 15 seconds and is searchable
A 'Videos Per Hour' bar chart displays clip creation counts grouped by hour using Recharts
Click tracking stat (total clip views/interactions) is displayed as a counter card
Support messaging form accepts a User ID and message text and sends via useSendAdminMessage hook

file_operations.description: Enhance AdminPanel to gate the entire component behind admin/owner role checks (use useGetOwnRole or useIsOwner hooks). Apply glassmorphism card styling to all sections. Ensure UserStatusManagement component is rendered with searchable user table, Ban/Unban toggles via useSetUserStatus hook, and batch clip deletion UI (e.g., checkboxes per user or per clip, with a 'Delete Selected Clips' button that calls the backend deleteClipsByUser method). Ensure ActivityLogTable is rendered with auto-refresh (polling every 15 seconds via useActivityLogs). Render a 'Videos Per Hour' bar chart using Recharts with data from a new hook or backend method (getVideosPerHour). Display a click tracking counter card showing total interactions via a new hook (useClickStats). Ensure AdminMessaging component is rendered for direct user messaging. Apply indigo accent colors to all interactive elements and buttons.

Enhance UserStatusManagement to add batch clip deletion functionality. For each user in the table, add a checkbox or 'Delete Clips' button that triggers a confirmation dialog and calls the backend deleteClipsByUser method via a new mutation hook (useDeleteClipsByUser). Apply glassmorphism card styling to the table container. Ensure Ban/Unban toggle buttons use indigo accent colors and update user status via useSetUserStatus hook.

Create a new SystemControls component that renders Restart and Shutdown action buttons for admin/owner users. Each button should trigger a confirmation dialog (using a modal or alert component) before calling the backend serverRestartAction() or serverShutdownAction() methods. Display the backend response message (success/error) using a toast notification. Apply glassmorphism card styling with indigo accent buttons. Use new mutation hooks (useServerRestart, useServerShutdown) to call the backend methods.

Enhance AppAnalytics to add a 'Videos Per Hour' bar chart using Recharts. Fetch data from a new hook (useVideosPerHour) that calls the backend getVideosPerHour() method. Display the chart in a glassmorphism card with indigo accent bars. Add a click tracking counter card that fetches data from a new hook (useClickStats) calling the backend getClickStats() method. Apply consistent dark-mode styling with indigo accents.

Create a new mutation hook useDeleteClipsByUser that calls the backend deleteClipsByUser(userId) method. Invalidate relevant query caches (clips, activity logs) on success and show toast notifications for success/error states.

Create a new mutation hook useServerRestart that calls the backend serverRestartAction() method. Show toast notification with the backend response message. Invalidate activity logs query cache on success.

Create a new mutation hook useServerShutdown that calls the backend serverShutdownAction() method. Show toast notification with the backend response message. Invalidate activity logs query cache on success.

Create a new query hook useVideosPerHour that fetches video upload statistics from the backend getVideosPerHour() method. Return an array of {hour: number, count: number} records for the last 24 hours. Enable the query only when the actor is available.

Create a new query hook useClickStats that fetches click/interaction statistics from the backend getClickStats() method. Return the total count as a number. Enable the query only when the actor is available.

Wire the AdminPanel component into the main app routing. Add a new route (e.g., /admin) that renders the AdminPanel component. Ensure the route is gated behind admin/owner role checks (use a route guard or conditional rendering based on useGetOwnRole). Add a navigation link to the Admin Panel in the SideNavigation and BottomNavigation components for admin/owner users only.
